hypothesis: Sprint 3 lever 4 — Original s3-a3 (full FT, LR=2e-5, leaked positive, 1 mined hard negative) was catastrophic (-0.212 nDCG@5). Was that an interaction with the lexical-leakage shortcut (s3-d1) or LR-too-hot (s3-d3), or are mined hard negatives independently bad for this corpus (false-negative pollution)? With leakage fixed (pos_conclusion_only) and LR fixed (1e-6), add 1 mined hard negative and see. If it helps or stays flat: BM25-mined hard negatives are usable when the recipe is right. If it regresses: false-negative pollution is real in this narrow legal corpus and we'd need either reranker-filtered negatives or BM25 mid-ranks (15-30).
